questions.text: What is a solute?

answers.text: The medium in which a substance is dissolved
Drug or other substance being dissolved (measured in mg)

questions.text: What is a solvent?

answers.text: Drug or other substance being dissolved
Medium in which solute is being dissolved (typically sterile water or saline; measured in mL)
A ratio of weight to volume

questions.text: When using the drug calculation formula, should you convert the percentage given in the problem?

answers.text: Yes, always convert to a decimal
No, do not convert the percentage given to you in the problem

questions.text: What is the concentration of a medication listed as 1:250?
To solve for percent concentration from a ratio (x:y), you should:

answers.text: Multiply x by y and divide by 100
Divide x by y and multiply by 100
Add x and y then multiply by 10
Subtract y from x and multiply by 100

questions.text: Short-Acting B2 Agonists (SABAs) are the medication of choice for what condition?
What is the action onset of Albuterol?
What is the action duration of Albuterol?
Which isomer of Albuterol is responsible for tremors and heart palpitations?
What advantage does Levalbuterol (Xopenex) have over Albuterol?

answers.text: No S isomer thus eliminating side effects seen with Albuterol

questions.text: What are the indications for Racemic Epinephrine?

answers.text: Inflammation of upper airway, post extubation stridor, epiglottitis, croup, bronchiolitis, bleeding (bronchoscopy)

questions.text: Epinephrine is the primary treatment for what condition?
What is the duration of Long-Acting Beta Agonists (LABAs)?
Salmeterol (Serevent) and Formoterol (Foradil/Perforomist) are known as your what drugs?
What are the indications for anticholinergic bronchodilators?

answers.text: Maintenance of COPD, emphysema and chronic bronchitis

questions.text: What is the duration of Ipratropium Bromide (Atrovent)?
Tiotropium Bromide (Spiriva) improves what measurement to assess lung function?
What is the duration of Tiotropium Bromide (Spiriva)?
Combivent and Duoneb are brand names for what combination?
Therapy is indicated in patients whose peak flow measures less than what percentage of their predicted PEF value?
How many times is the peak flow measurement repeated?
Which effort is recorded in the patient's chart for PEF?
A PEF of 60%–80% of personal best falls in what zone?
A PEF less than 60% of personal best (Red Zone) requires what actions?

answers.text: Self-administration of bronchodilator, contacting the physician, and calling 9-1-1
Self-administration of bronchodilator plus possibly oral steroids

questions.text: Theophylline is contraindicated for what?
What toxic effects of Theophylline must be monitored?

answers.text: Nausea, arrhythmias, seizures; liver/kidney functions monitored

questions.text: What is Caffeine Citrate (Cafcit) indicated for?

answers.text: Apnea of Prematurity (AOP) in premature infants

questions.text: SABAs are described as the most effective medications for what?
